mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, it signals a decelerating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when UNRATE crosses 4.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates synchronized demand across business and consumer sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series trend upward, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 244.0, it signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses 244.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength that boosts household income and corporate revenue, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real new house sales and housing starts confirms a self-reinforcing housing cycle that amplifies consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant or flips sign when housing inventory constraints emerge, or predictive accuracy drops below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index during periods of historically low unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output growth and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when UNRATE is below 4.2%, or the mechanism loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in supply-constrained regimes.
